name: Paperclip
description: Paperclip is an open-source Node.js + React platform for orchestrating AI agent teams to run businesses autonomously. Launched March 2026, it crossed 43K GitHub stars in weeks. Solves the 'runaway spend' problem with atomic task execution and budget enforcement. Supports multiple businesses in one install with complete data isolation.
oneLiner: Open-source orchestration platform for zero-human companies — org charts, budgets, governance, and AI agent coordination.
funding: Open-source project. No disclosed venture funding.
pricing: Open Source (free, self-hosted). No Paperclip account required.
capabilities: Bring Your Own Agent (BYOA) — works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini CLI, OpenClaw, Pi, OpenCode, Hermes
Org chart with role-based agent hierarchy and reporting lines
Atomic task checkout and budget enforcement — no double-work, no runaway spend
Goal alignment — every task traces back to company mission
Heartbeat scheduling with delegation flows up and down org chart
Multi-company support with complete data isolation
Ticket system with threaded conversations and tool-call tracing
Board-level governance — approve hires, override strategy, pause/terminate agents
Plugin framework and SDK for extensibility
Company import/export with secret scrubbing (companies.sh)
Routines engine for recurring tasks with triggers
Execution workspaces with lifecycle management
Skills manager — company-scoped skills with agent sync
Embedded PostgreSQL with single npx command onboarding
Mobile-ready PWA interface
Immutable audit log for every conversation and decision
AI employee orchestration — hire agents, set goals, automate business
Multi-business support with data isolation
Atomic task execution with budget enforcement
Full tracing and audit trail for all agent actions
Ticket-based agent communication system

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: Web-based React UI that looks like a project management tool. Define company structure (org chart, departments, roles), set budgets and goals, then assign AI agents to roles. Agents pick up tasks, execute within budget constraints, and report back. The interface surfaces agent activity, spend, and goal progress in real-time dashboards.
strengths: Novel 'company operating system' paradigm resonates with the zero-human company vision
Atomic budget enforcement prevents runaway agent spend
Context resumption across heartbeats — agents don't restart from scratch
Fully self-hosted with no vendor lock-in or account requirement
Explosive community growth (42K+ stars in weeks) validates demand
Open-source transparency enables audit and customization
Task checkout prevents duplicate work across agents
weaknesses: Very early stage (released March 2026) — expect rapid breaking changes
No enterprise features (SSO, RBAC, compliance, SOC2)
No managed cloud offering — self-hosted only
No native integrations with enterprise systems (CRM, ERP)
Governance model untested at enterprise scale
notableFeatures: Org chart-based agent hierarchy — agents have roles, managers, and reporting lines
Atomic task checkout and budget enforcement — no double-work, no runaway spend
Agent heartbeat with context resumption — stateful across interruptions
Goal alignment framework with OKR-style tracking
Zero vendor dependency — fully self-hosted, no account needed

deploymentOptions.notes: Self-hosted only. Node.js + React stack. No managed cloud offering.

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.rationale: Web UI for org/role definition. Configuration-driven, not visual builder. Functional but early-stage polish.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Self-hosted setup requires Node.js knowledge. Templates and examples available but limited.

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.rationale: Dashboard shows agent activity and task status. No dedicated debugging or trace tooling yet.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: README and community guides. Rapidly evolving docs. Medium/DEV Community tutorials supplement official docs.

subCriteria.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
subCriteria.rationale: Core differentiator — org chart hierarchy, role-based coordination, task delegation, and inter-agent protocols.

subCriteria.name: Human-in-the-Loop Design
subCriteria.rationale: Dashboard for monitoring and intervention. Budget gates serve as implicit approval mechanism. No structured escalation.

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.rationale: Task management with assignment. No pre-built workflow library. Community examples filling the gap.

subCriteria.name: Team Development
subCriteria.rationale: Org chart, department structure, and role definitions provide strong team scaffolding. Goal alignment with OKRs.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.rationale: No pre-built enterprise connectors. Agents use their own tools. Integration is agent-level, not platform-level.

subCriteria.name: API & Protocol Standards
subCriteria.rationale: Node.js API for agent management. No MCP, GraphQL, or standardized protocols.

subCriteria.name: Authentication & Access
subCriteria.rationale: Basic self-hosted auth. No SSO, SAML, or enterprise IAM.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: No built-in RAG, vector store, or data pipeline. Agents manage their own data access.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.rationale: Budget enforcement acts as financial guardrail. Task checkout prevents double-work. No behavioral guardrails.

subCriteria.name: Audit Trail & Compliance
subCriteria.rationale: Agent activity logging. Budget tracking. No compliance certifications.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: Org chart hierarchy provides implicit access control. No granular RBAC system.

subCriteria.name: Explainability & Transparency
subCriteria.rationale: Open-source code provides full transparency. Dashboard shows agent reasoning and decisions.

subCriteria.name: Deployment Flexibility
subCriteria.rationale: Self-hosted only. Node.js + React stack. No managed cloud offering.

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.rationale: Single-server deployment. No horizontal scaling or enterprise monitoring.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Budget tracking for agents. No LLM cost management or optimization.

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.rationale: 42K+ GitHub stars in weeks. Massive community interest. Very early-stage ecosystem.

scoringRationale: 52/100 overall. Strongest: Orchestration (15/20). Weakest: Operations (6/20). Breakdown: Creation (12/20), Orchestration (15/20), Integration (7/20), Governance (12/20), Operations (6/20).
targetUser: Developers and entrepreneurs building fully autonomous AI-run businesses

communityBuzz: 42K+ GitHub stars in under a month — fastest-growing agent project of 2026
'2025 was the year of the AI employee. 2026 is the year of the AI company' — common framing in coverage
DEV Community post 'How We Built a Company Powered by 14 AI Agents' went viral
Praised for atomic budget enforcement — 'no runaway spend' resonates with enterprise concerns
Skepticism about 'zero-human company' viability in regulated industries

metrics.value: 8+ (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini CLI, OpenClaw, Pi, OpenCode, Hermes)

steps.title: Define Your Company Structure
steps.description: Set up a virtual company in Paperclip with departments and roles — a Research division, a Content team, and a QA department. Define each role's responsibilities, tools, and decision authority. The org chart becomes your agent topology.

works: Org-chart-based agent hierarchy is a genuinely novel paradigm — instantly understandable
CEO/department/worker agent topology mirrors real organizational thinking
Budget enforcement per department prevents runaway costs
doesntWork: Documentation is sparse — early-stage project with gaps
Org chart becomes unwieldy for complex organizations — no grouping or nesting
No visual org chart editor — must configure via code
pmCritique.userPsychology: The org chart metaphor is a brilliant hook — users immediately understand the vision. But the gap between the concept and current implementation maturity creates disappointment when you try to build something real.

steps.title: Set Goals and Resource Budgets
steps.description: Define company-level objectives: 'Produce 20 industry analysis reports per week.' Set token budgets per department, cost limits per task, and quality thresholds. Paperclip's governance engine enforces these constraints across all agent activity.

works: Inter-agent communication protocol is well-designed — clear message passing
Task delegation from CEO agent to department agents is intuitive
Budget tracking dashboard shows spend per agent role
doesntWork: Message passing has no priority system — urgent tasks wait in queue
No retry or escalation policy for failed agent communications
Dashboard updates are not real-time — manual refresh required
pmCritique.userPsychology: The delegation model satisfies the desire for control — you are the CEO of an AI company. But the lack of priority and escalation means the system feels more like email than a responsive organization.

steps.title: Agents Self-Organize and Execute
steps.description: Agents autonomously pick up tasks from the goal queue, coordinate with teammates, and execute — researchers gather data, writers draft reports, QA agents review for accuracy. Inter-agent communication follows your org hierarchy.

works: Worker agents execute tasks with clear input/output contracts
Parallel execution across departments when tasks are independent
Execution logs per agent role aid debugging
doesntWork: Worker agents have no learning — same mistakes repeat across runs
No shared knowledge base between departments
Error recovery requires restarting the entire org, not just the failed agent
pmCritique.userPsychology: Seeing multiple agents work simultaneously is exciting and validates the multi-agent vision, but the lack of cross-department learning makes the organization feel like independent contractors rather than a team.

steps.title: Monitor Company Operations
steps.description: Track real-time company performance on the dashboard — tasks completed, quality scores, cost per deliverable, and department utilization. Identify bottlenecks, underperforming agents, and budget overruns before they compound.

works: Budget alerts prevent unexpected costs before they happen
Per-department cost attribution is clear and actionable
doesntWork: Budget optimization suggestions are absent — just tracking, not advising
Cannot set budget policies (e.g., auto-pause at threshold)
No forecasting based on historical usage patterns
pmCritique.userPsychology: Cost visibility is the grown-up feature that separates serious tools from demos. Users who see exactly what each agent costs trust the system more — even when costs are high, transparency builds confidence.

steps.title: Govern and Scale
steps.description: Add new roles, adjust budgets, and refine agent instructions as the company evolves. Paperclip's governance model ensures agents operate within defined boundaries — no rogue spending, no unauthorized actions, full audit trail.

works: Full self-hosted deployment means no data leaves your infrastructure
Docker Compose setup gets you running quickly
doesntWork: Self-hosted only means you are responsible for scaling and reliability
No monitoring or alerting out of the box — must bring your own
Community support only — no enterprise support tier available
pmCritique.userPsychology: Open-source true believers love the self-hosted model, but it creates a divide: hobbyists are excited, enterprise teams are skeptical because there is no one to call when production breaks at 3am.

pmVerdict.summary: Paperclip — limited public demo content available. Verdict based on documentation and existing research data only. Score reflects capability claims that could not be verified through video walkthrough.
strengths: See existing capability analysis in the product profile
risks: No video demo available for verification
Product capabilities based on documentation only
UX quality unverifiable without live demo

ctoVerdict.summary: Insufficient public demo material to evaluate Paperclip for enterprise adoption. Recommend requesting a live demo from the vendor before making any commitment.
strengths: See existing scoring for capability assessment
dealBreakers: Cannot verify UX claims without walkthrough

youtubeReviews.title: Paperclip AI Just Changed Agent Orchestration: Overview for Beginners
Paperclip AI Just Changed Everything — Zero-Human Companies

coreCompetence.bestAt: The boldest vision in the space — org-chart-based multi-agent orchestration designed to run entire companies with zero human employees.
coreCompetence.targetUser: Visionary founders and experimenters exploring fully autonomous AI organizations. Builders who want to architect agent teams that mirror human organizational structures.
coreCompetence.vsAlternatives: Wins on conceptual ambition — no other platform explicitly targets zero-human companies. Unique org-chart agent topology. Loses on production maturity — early stage, limited documentation. Loses to established platforms on enterprise readiness.
